@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: e380
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Virtual scrolling / transform animations: rects registered during a CSS transform animation get stale coordinates'
+---
+## What
+
+Follow-up to `01KPVTKZ1VGDSBB0HPYTTAHJNH` (scroll-ancestor rect re-report, commit `33f60d132`). That fix handles **scroll events**. This task handles **CSS transform animations** — a different vector that hits every `FocusScope` inside a sliding / fading / morphing ancestor.
+
+### Concrete repro site
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/slide-panel.tsx:37` uses Tailwind `translate-x-0` / `translate-x-full` for its open/close animation:
+
+```tsx
+className={cn(
+  "fixed top-0 right-0 bottom-0 z-30 w-[380px] bg-background border-l border-border shadow-xl transition-transform duration-200",
+  open ? "translate-x-0" : "translate-x-full",
+)}
+```
+
+Every `<InspectorFocusBridge>` is wrapped in a `<SlidePanel>`. When the user opens an inspector:
+
+1. Panel starts at `translate-x-full` (off-screen right).
+2. Class flip triggers `transition-transform duration-200` → panel slides to `translate-x-0`.
+3. Inside the panel, `InspectorFocusBridge` mounts. `<FocusLayer>` pushes. `<FocusScope>` field rows mount, their `useRectObserver` effects fire, each calls `getBoundingClientRect()` and `invoke("spatial_register", {x, y, w, h, ...})`.
+
+**The mount-time `report()` fires during step 3, while the panel is still mid-animation.** The rect `getBoundingClientRect()` returns reflects the panel's current (mid-slide) transform, not its final position. `ResizeObserver` doesn't fire when the transform completes (size didn't change). There is no scroll during the animation, so `01KPVTKZ1VGDSBB0HPYTTAHJNH`'s scroll listener doesn't trigger either. **Rust ends up with inspector-field rects at stale mid-animation x-coordinates forever.**
+
+### Why this matches the live symptom
+
+Task `01KPVT4K538CJHJR31NNQHY8EH` reports that `nav.down` past the last inspector field leaks focus to a window-layer card. The algorithm is provably correct (6 unit + 5 integration tests all green). The missing piece is: the INSPECTOR fields' rects, registered during the slide-in animation, have wrong x-coordinates that bleed geometrically into the background layer's area. Beam-test scoring from the "last" inspector field (whose rect is at some pre-animation x) picks a neighbor below — and if that neighbor's rect is a window-layer card at similar geometry, Rust's layer filter correctly culls it. But if the inspector-field rect is PAST the window-layer card rect geometrically, the beam test's "next in direction" resolution can behave weirdly.
+
+Even if this doesn't explain the layer-escape exactly, it's a concrete staleness bug regardless — the inspector's spatial coordinates don't match reality for the 200ms animation window, and depending on when the user presses a nav key, weird things happen.
+
+### Fix direction
+
+Two complementary mechanisms:
+
+1. **Listen for `transitionend` on animated ancestors.** When an ancestor of the observed element fires `transitionend` for a `transform` / `translate` / position property, re-run `report()`. Attach a bubbling `transitionend` listener at `document` or at the scroll-ancestor level (walks up), filter by `propertyName`. Cleanup on unmount.
+
+2. **Re-report on the first RAF AFTER mount.** Even without `transitionend`, a single-RAF-deferred `report()` on mount catches the common case where layout settles one frame after the observer's initial callback. Cost: one extra `invoke` per FocusScope on mount. Worth it if it eliminates the entire class of stale-initial-rect bugs.
+
+Both are cheap. Combine them.
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` — extend `useRectObserver`:
+  - Add a `transitionend` listener on `document` (or bubble-scoped to scroll ancestor), filter by `propertyName` in `["transform", "translate", "left", "top", "right", "bottom"]`, call `report()`.
+  - Schedule a `requestAnimationFrame` after the initial `report()` call that re-invokes `report()`. Cancel on unmount.
+
+### Files to add
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-nav-transition-rect.test.tsx` — chromium browser test:
+  - Mount a FocusScope inside a `<div style={{ transform: 'translateX(100%)', transition: 'transform 100ms' }}>` that animates to `translateX(0)` after mount.
+  - Capture initial `spatial_register` invoke's rect.
+  - Dispatch `transitionend` event (or wait 120ms for animation to complete).
+  - Assert a second `spatial_register` invoke fires with a rect whose x is the post-animation value.
+  - Must FAIL against HEAD (only initial stale rect lands) and PASS after the fix.
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope-transition.node.test.ts` — happy-dom unit test for the ancestor-walk logic if we add one. If the listener is just `document.addEventListener("transitionend", ...)`, no ancestor walk is needed and this file can be skipped.
+
+### Out of scope
+
+- Changing SlidePanel's animation to not use transform. Transform is the right CSS primitive; the observer must adapt.
+- Implementing `MutationObserver`-based transform detection. Transitionend + RAF-on-mount covers the practical cases and is cheaper.
+- Inspector-specific fix. The mechanism must be general — any consumer (modals, drawers, animated sidebars) benefits automatically.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `useRectObserver` attaches a `transitionend` listener that re-runs `report()` when a transform-class property finishes animating on any ancestor
+- [ ] `useRectObserver` also re-runs `report()` once on the frame after initial mount (RAF-deferred) to catch layout-settle races
+- [ ] Cleanup removes the `transitionend` listener and cancels the RAF on unmount
+- [ ] Browser test: mount a FocusScope inside an animated ancestor, confirm a second `spatial_register` invoke fires with post-animation coordinates — fails on HEAD, passes after the fix
+- [ ] Existing tests (scroll re-report, ResizeObserver, mount/unmount contracts) still green
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all green
+- [ ] No new permanent `console.warn` traces in production code
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- spatial-nav-transition-rect` — passes
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- focus-scope` — existing tests green
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- spatial-nav-virtual-scroll` — previous scroll-listener tests still green (regression guard)
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — full suite green
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Write the failing transition-rect browser test first. The test must fail on HEAD with only one `spatial_register` invoke (the stale mount-time one) and pass after the fix emits a second invoke with post-animation coordinates.
+- Do NOT change SlidePanel, entity-inspector, or any consumer. The fix is general in `useRectObserver`.
+- RAF throttling from the scroll-listener fix applies here too — coalesce multiple transitionend events inside a single frame into one re-report.
+- Keep the mechanism symmetrical with the scroll-listener: both listeners live in the same `useEffect`, share the same RAF token, same cleanup. Don't fork the code paths.

changes.value: Virtual scrolling / transform animations: rects registered during a CSS transform animation get stale coordinates

## What

Follow-up to `01KPVTKZ1VGDSBB0HPYTTAHJNH` (scroll-ancestor rect re-report, commit `33f60d132`). That fix handles **scroll events**. This task handles **CSS transform animations** — a different vector that hits every `FocusScope` inside a sliding / fading / morphing ancestor.

### Concrete repro site

`kanban-app/ui/src/components/slide-panel.tsx:37` uses Tailwind `translate-x-0` / `translate-x-full` for its open/close animation:

```tsx
className={cn(
  "fixed top-0 right-0 bottom-0 z-30 w-[380px] bg-background border-l border-border shadow-xl transition-transform duration-200",
  open ? "translate-x-0" : "translate-x-full",
)}
```

Every `<InspectorFocusBridge>` is wrapped in a `<SlidePanel>`. When the user opens an inspector:

1. Panel starts at `translate-x-full` (off-screen right).
2. Class flip triggers `transition-transform duration-200` → panel slides to `translate-x-0`.
3. Inside the panel, `InspectorFocusBridge` mounts. `<FocusLayer>` pushes. `<FocusScope>` field rows mount, their `useRectObserver` effects fire, each calls `getBoundingClientRect()` and `invoke("spatial_register", {x, y, w, h, ...})`.

**The mount-time `report()` fires during step 3, while the panel is still mid-animation.** The rect `getBoundingClientRect()` returns reflects the panel's current (mid-slide) transform, not its final position. `ResizeObserver` doesn't fire when the transform completes (size didn't change). There is no scroll during the animation, so `01KPVTKZ1VGDSBB0HPYTTAHJNH`'s scroll listener doesn't trigger either. **Rust ends up with inspector-field rects at stale mid-animation x-coordinates forever.**

### Why this matches the live symptom

Task `01KPVT4K538CJHJR31NNQHY8EH` reports that `nav.down` past the last inspector field leaks focus to a window-layer card. The algorithm is provably correct (6 unit + 5 integration tests all green). The missing piece is: the INSPECTOR fields' rects, registered during the slide-in animation, have wrong x-coordinates that bleed geometrically into the background layer's area. Beam-test scoring from the "last" inspector field (whose rect is at some pre-animation x) picks a neighbor below — and if that neighbor's rect is a window-layer card at similar geometry, Rust's layer filter correctly culls it. But if the inspector-field rect is PAST the window-layer card rect geometrically, the beam test's "next in direction" resolution can behave weirdly.

Even if this doesn't explain the layer-escape exactly, it's a concrete staleness bug regardless — the inspector's spatial coordinates don't match reality for the 200ms animation window, and depending on when the user presses a nav key, weird things happen.

### Fix direction

Two complementary mechanisms:

1. **Listen for `transitionend` on animated ancestors.** When an ancestor of the observed element fires `transitionend` for a `transform` / `translate` / position property, re-run `report()`. Attach a bubbling `transitionend` listener at `document` or at the scroll-ancestor level (walks up), filter by `propertyName`. Cleanup on unmount.

2. **Re-report on the first RAF AFTER mount.** Even without `transitionend`, a single-RAF-deferred `report()` on mount catches the common case where layout settles one frame after the observer's initial callback. Cost: one extra `invoke` per FocusScope on mount. Worth it if it eliminates the entire class of stale-initial-rect bugs.

Both are cheap. Combine them.

### Files to modify

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` — extend `useRectObserver`:
  - Add a `transitionend` listener on `document` (or bubble-scoped to scroll ancestor), filter by `propertyName` in `["transform", "translate", "left", "top", "right", "bottom"]`, call `report()`.
  - Schedule a `requestAnimationFrame` after the initial `report()` call that re-invokes `report()`. Cancel on unmount.

### Files to add

- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-nav-transition-rect.test.tsx` — chromium browser test:
  - Mount a FocusScope inside a `<div style={{ transform: 'translateX(100%)', transition: 'transform 100ms' }}>` that animates to `translateX(0)` after mount.
  - Capture initial `spatial_register` invoke's rect.
  - Dispatch `transitionend` event (or wait 120ms for animation to complete).
  - Assert a second `spatial_register` invoke fires with a rect whose x is the post-animation value.
  - Must FAIL against HEAD (only initial stale rect lands) and PASS after the fix.

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope-transition.node.test.ts` — happy-dom unit test for the ancestor-walk logic if we add one. If the listener is just `document.addEventListener("transitionend", ...)`, no ancestor walk is needed and this file can be skipped.

### Out of scope

- Changing SlidePanel's animation to not use transform. Transform is the right CSS primitive; the observer must adapt.
- Implementing `MutationObserver`-based transform detection. Transitionend + RAF-on-mount covers the practical cases and is cheaper.
- Inspector-specific fix. The mechanism must be general — any consumer (modals, drawers, animated sidebars) benefits automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `useRectObserver` attaches a `transitionend` listener that re-runs `report()` when a transform-class property finishes animating on any ancestor
- [ ] `useRectObserver` also re-runs `report()` once on the frame after initial mount (RAF-deferred) to catch layout-settle races
- [ ] Cleanup removes the `transitionend` listener and cancels the RAF on unmount
- [ ] Browser test: mount a FocusScope inside an animated ancestor, confirm a second `spatial_register` invoke fires with post-animation coordinates — fails on HEAD, passes after the fix
- [ ] Existing tests (scroll re-report, ResizeObserver, mount/unmount contracts) still green
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all green
- [ ] No new permanent `console.warn` traces in production code

## Tests

- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- spatial-nav-transition-rect` — passes
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- focus-scope` — existing tests green
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- spatial-nav-virtual-scroll` — previous scroll-listener tests still green (regression guard)
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — full suite green

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd`. Write the failing transition-rect browser test first. The test must fail on HEAD with only one `spatial_register` invoke (the stale mount-time one) and pass after the fix emits a second invoke with post-animation coordinates.
- Do NOT change SlidePanel, entity-inspector, or any consumer. The fix is general in `useRectObserver`.
- RAF throttling from the scroll-listener fix applies here too — coalesce multiple transitionend events inside a single frame into one re-report.
- Keep the mechanism symmetrical with the scroll-listener: both listeners live in the same `useEffect`, share the same RAF token, same cleanup. Don't fork the code paths.

There is no scroll during the animation, so `01KPVTKZ1VGDSBB0HPYTTAHJNH`'s scroll listener doesn't trigger either. **Rust ends up with inspector-field rects at stale mid-animation x-coordinates forever.**

+### Why this matches the live symptom

-Extended `useRectObserver` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`:
+Task `01KPVT4K538CJHJR31NNQHY8EH` reports that `nav.down` past the last inspector field leaks focus to a window-layer card. The algorithm is provably correct (6 unit + 5 integration tests all green). The missing piece is: the INSPECTOR fields' rects, registered during the slide-in animation, have wrong x-coordinates that bleed geometrically into the background layer's area. Beam-test scoring from the "last" inspector field (whose rect is at some pre-animation x) picks a neighbor below — and if that neighbor's rect is a window-layer card at similar geometry, Rust's layer filter correctly culls it. But if the inspector-field rect is PAST the window-layer card rect geometrically, the beam test's "next in direction" resolution can behave weirdly.

-1. Added a `POSITIONAL_TRANSITION_PROPS` allowlist (`transform`, `translate`, `left`, `top`, `right`, `bottom`).
-2. Added a `transitionend` listener on `document` that filters by `propertyName` and triggers a RAF-throttled `report()`.
-3. Added a RAF-deferred re-report one frame after the initial `report()` call — catches layout-settle races even when no CSS transition fires.
-4. Unified the RAF token across the scroll listener, the transition listener, and the mount-deferred re-report — any signal within one frame coalesces into a single `spatial_register` invocation.
-5. Cleanup tears down the listener, cancels the pending RAF, and preserves the existing ResizeObserver + scroll listener teardown.
+Even if this doesn't explain the layer-escape exactly, it's a concrete staleness bug regardless — the inspector's spatial coordinates don't match reality for the 200ms animation window, and depending on when the user presses a nav key, weird things happen.

-### Files modified
+### Fix direction

-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` — extended `useRectObserver` as above.
+Two complementary mechanisms:
+
+1. **Listen for `transitionend` on animated ancestors.** When an ancestor of the observed element fires `transitionend` for a `transform` / `translate` / position property, re-run `report()`. Attach a bubbling `transitionend` listener at `document` or at the scroll-ancestor level (walks up), filter by `propertyName`. Cleanup on unmount.
+
+2. **Re-report on the first RAF AFTER mount.** Even without `transitionend`, a single-RAF-deferred `report()` on mount catches the common case where layout settles one frame after the observer's initial callback. Cost: one extra `invoke` per FocusScope on mount. Worth it if it eliminates the entire class of stale-initial-rect bugs.
+
+Both are cheap. Combine them.

+### Files to modify

For a first-load app with 100–200 scopes that is 100–200 extra Tauri IPCs on cold start. The trade-off is deliberate — the hook cannot cheaply tell at mount time whether an animated ancestor is in flight, so the deferred re-report happens unconditionally rather than gating behind a brittle heuristic.  5. Cleanup tears down the listener, cancels the pending RAF, and preserves the existing ResizeObserver + scroll listener teardown.
